sourceText: Early in his career, Eastman faced financial struggles and significant technical hurdles in developing a practical photographic system for amateurs. The complexity and cost of existing photography limited its appeal.
translatedText: A farkon aikinsa, Eastman ya fuskanci gwagwarmayar kudi da kuma manyan matsalolin fasaha wajen haɓaka tsarin daukar hoto mai amfani ga masu son. Haɗin kai da farashin ɗaukar hoto na yanzu sun iyakance roƙonsa.

sourceText: He overcame these challenges by inventing flexible roll film and a simple, affordable camera. His brilliant marketing strategy, centered around the Kodak name and the 'You press the button, we do the rest' slogan, made photography accessible and immensely popular worldwide.
translatedText: Ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen ta hanyar ƙirƙirar fim ɗin nadi mai sassauƙa da kyamara mai sauƙi, mai araha. Kyakkyawan dabarun tallan sa, wanda ya shafi sunan Kodak da taken 'Kuna danna maballin, muna yin sauran' taken, ya sanya daukar hoto ya zama mai isa ga kuma shahararriya a duk duniya.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Elisha Otis
translatedText: Elisha Otis

sourceText: Early in his career, Otis faced financial struggles and technical hurdles. A significant challenge was the perceived danger of elevators, which he aimed to solve with his safety brake, a crucial invention for his business's viability.
translatedText: A farkon aikinsa, Otis ya fuskanci gwagwarmayar kudi da matsalolin fasaha. Babban ƙalubale shi ne hasashe haɗarin lif, wanda ya yi niyya don warwarewa da birki na aminci, wani muhimmin ƙirƙira don ci gaban kasuwancinsa.

sourceText: Driven by his vision for a safer elevator, Otis persevered through numerous prototypes and tests. His pivotal moment came at the 1854 New York World's Fair, where he dramatically demonstrated the reliability of his safety brake by having the elevator platform cut while he stood on it, proving its effectiveness and winning public confidence.
translatedText: Sakamakon hangen nesansa don samun ingantacciyar lif, Otis ya dage ta hanyar samfura da gwaje-gwaje masu yawa. Muhimmin lokacinsa ya zo ne a Baje kolin Duniya na New York a 1854, inda ya nuna matuƙar matuƙar amincin amincinsa ta hanyar yanke dandalin lif yayin da ya tsaya akansa, yana tabbatar da ingancinsa da samun amincewar jama'a.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Jang Yeong-sil
translatedText: Jang Yeong-sil

sourceText: Discrimination and difficulties faced due to his low-born status as a skilled craftsman. The limitations of not being able to attain higher office despite his achievements, solely due to his birth.
translatedText: Wariya da wahalhalu da ake fuskanta saboda matsayinsa na ɗan ƙasa a matsayinsa na ƙwararren mai sana'a. Iyaka na rashin iya samun babban mukami duk da nasarorin da ya samu, saboda haihuwarsa kawai.

sourceText: He overcame these challenges by proving his worth through exceptional talent and relentless effort, earning the king's trust and dedicating himself to the advancement of science and technology.
translatedText: Ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen ta hanyar tabbatar da kimarsa ta hanyar hazaka na musamman da ƙoƙari marar iyaka, ya sami amincewar sarki tare da sadaukar da kansa ga ci gaban kimiyya da fasaha.

sourceText: Joseon Dynasty
translatedText: Daular Joseon

sourceText: Yun Dong-ju
translatedText: Yun Dong-ju

sourceText: He grappled with the dark reality of the Japanese colonial era, striving to sublimate the nation's suffering and his own inner turmoil into poetry. He constantly reflected amidst oppressive circumstances and personal solitude.
translatedText: Ya yi kokawa da duhun gaskiyar zamanin mulkin mallaka na Japan, yana mai fafutukar kawar da wahalhalun da al'ummar kasar ke ciki da kuma rudanin da yake ciki a cikin wakoki. Ya kasance yana nunawa a cikin yanayi na zalunci da kadaici.

sourceText: Through his poetry, he sought inner peace by expressing his struggles and the nation's pain. Even in despair, he never lost hope, creating a timeless body of work by singing of pure beauty, like the stars.
translatedText: Ta hanyar wakarsa, ya nemi kwanciyar hankali ta hanyar bayyana gwagwarmayarsa da radadin al'umma. Ko da a cikin fidda rai, bai taba rasa bege ba, yana haifar da wani aiki maras lokaci ta hanyar rera kyawawan kyawawan abubuwa, kamar taurari.

sourceText: Japanese Colonial Period
translatedText: Zaman Mulkin Jafananci

sourceText: As a man of equestrian rank, Cicero constantly faced scrutiny and opposition as he ascended within the Roman elite. His political clashes with powerful figures like Julius Caesar and Mark Antony posed significant threats to his life and career. In his later years, political turmoil forced him into exile.
translatedText: A matsayinsa na ɗan dawaki, Cicero yakan fuskanci bincike da adawa yayin da ya hau cikin ƙwararrun 'yan Romawa. Rikicin siyasarsa tare da manyan mutane kamar Julius Caesar da Mark Antony sun haifar da babbar barazana ga rayuwarsa da aikinsa. A cikin shekarunsa na baya, rikice-rikicen siyasa sun tilasta masa yin hijira.

sourceText: Cicero's sharp intellect, masterful oratory, and unwavering dedication to the Republic allowed him to navigate these challenges. Through his philosophical works and speeches, he inspired Roman citizens with the virtues of republicanism, justice, and law. Though his political endeavors met with setbacks, his intellectual legacy endures.
translatedText: Hikimar Cicero, ƙwararriyar magana, da sadaukar da kai ga Jamhuriya sun ba shi damar gudanar da waɗannan ƙalubalen. Ta wurin ayyukansa na falsafa da jawabansa, ya zaburar da ’yan ƙasar Roma da kyawawan halaye na jamhuriya, adalci, da doka. Ko da yake kokarinsa na siyasa ya gamu da koma baya, abin da ya gada na hankali ya dore.

sourceText: 1st Century BC
translatedText: Karni na 1 BC

sourceText: He faced accusations from his own sons of attempting to usurp his property, which he overcame by demonstrating his mental faculties in old age. He also navigated the tumultuous political landscape of Athens, witnessing its eventual decline.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci tuhuma daga 'ya'yansa na yunkurin kwace masa kadarorinsa, wanda ya ci nasara ta hanyar nuna tunaninsa a lokacin tsufa. Ya kuma zagaya yanayin siyasar Athens mai cike da hargitsi, yana shaida faduwarta daga ƙarshe.

sourceText: Sophocles navigated these challenges through his wisdom and unparalleled artistic achievements. His dramatic prowess earned him widespread acclaim and deep respect from the Athenian citizenry.
translatedText: Sophocles ya kewaya waɗannan ƙalubalen ta hanyar hikimarsa da nasarorin fasaha marasa misaltuwa. Bajintar da ya yi ta sa ya sami yabo da kuma girmama shi sosai daga mutanen Athens.

sourceText: 5th Century BC
translatedText: Karni na 5 BC

sourceText: The process of systematizing mathematical truths might have led to criticism or misunderstanding from contemporaries. Furthermore, the scarcity of biographical records makes it difficult to ascertain personal hardships.
translatedText: Tsarin tsara gaskiyar ilimin lissafi na iya haifar da zargi ko rashin fahimta daga mutanen zamani. Bugu da ƙari, ƙarancin bayanan tarihin rayuwa yana sa yana da wahala a iya gano wahalhalun da ke cikin mutum.

sourceText: Euclid overcame these challenges through rigorous proofs and an axiomatic system. His treatise 'Elements' became the standard for mathematical education for millennia, and his logical approach profoundly influenced posterity.
translatedText: Euclid ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen ta hanyar kwararan hujjoji da tsarin axiomatic. Rubutunsa 'Elements' ya zama ma'auni na ilimin lissafi na shekaru dubu, kuma dabararsa ta hankali ta yi tasiri sosai ga zuriya.

sourceText: 4th - 3rd Century BC
translatedText: Karni na 4-3 BC

sourceText: The isolation of fighting against the overwhelming military power of Portugal and the ultimate inability to prevent the fall of his empire.
translatedText: Keɓewar yaƙi da ƙarfin soja na Portugal da kuma rashin iyawar ƙarshe don hana faduwar daularsa.

sourceText: Even after his capture, he remained a symbol of resistance for his people, and his story became part of Mozambique's spirit of independence.
translatedText: Ko bayan kama shi, ya kasance alama ce ta juriya ga jama'arsa, kuma labarinsa ya zama wani bangare na ruhin 'yancin kai na Mozambique.

sourceText: Late 19th Century
translatedText: Marigayi karni na 19

sourceText: Nana Asma'u
translatedText: Nana Asma'u

sourceText: Witnessing the devastation of war during the Fulani Jihad and the widespread lack of education for rural women.
translatedText: Shaidawa barnar yaki a lokacin Jihadin Fulani da kuma yadda matan karkara ke fama da matsalar karancin ilimi.

sourceText: Using her position, scholarship, and poetry to create a sustainable educational system for women that spread knowledge and Islamic values.
translatedText: Yin amfani da matsayinta, karatunta, da kuma wakokinta don samar da ingantaccen tsarin ilimi ga mata wanda ke yada ilimi da dabi'un Musulunci.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Facing immense resistance from the powerful priesthood and the populace for abandoning Egypt's traditional pantheon of gods in favor of the one god, the Aten.
translatedText: Fuskantar juriya mai girma daga rukunin firistoci masu ƙarfi da kuma jama'a don yin watsi da al'adar gumaka ta Masar don goyon bayan allah ɗaya, Aten.

sourceText: Co-ruling with Akhenaten, we founded a new capital, Akhetaten (Amarna), and fostered a revolutionary new style of art and worship to solidify our vision for the Aten.
translatedText: Tare da yin mulki tare da Akhenaten, mun kafa sabon babban birni, Akhetaten (Amarna), kuma mun haɓaka sabon salon fasaha da bautar juyin juya hali don ƙarfafa hangen nesanmu game da Aten.

sourceText: New Kingdom of Egypt
translatedText: Sabuwar Masarautar Masar

sourceText: The murder of her husband by her own brother, Pygmalion, and the profound betrayal and abandonment by her lover, Aeneas.
translatedText: Kisan mijinta da ɗan'uwanta, Pygmalion, da kuma babban cin amana da watsi da masoyinta, Aeneas.

sourceText: She channeled her grief into action, founding the great city of Carthage and becoming a capable leader. Faced with a second tragedy, she chose to preserve her honor through a defiant death.
translatedText: Ta gabatar da bakin cikinta cikin aiki, ta kafa babban birnin Carthage kuma ta zama jagora mai ƙwazo. Ta fuskanci wani bala'i na biyu, ta zaɓi ta kiyaye mutuncinta ta hanyar mutuwa.

sourceText: Ancient Carthage (c. 8th Century BCE)
translatedText: Tsohon Carthage (C. 8th Century KZ)

sourceText: Ranavalona I
translatedText: Ranavalona I

sourceText: Constant threats from European colonial powers and internal dissent
translatedText: Barazana akai-akai daga turawan mulkin mallaka da rashin yarda na cikin gida

sourceText: Enforcing strict isolationism, persecuting Christians, and consolidating power through the tangena ordeal
translatedText: Ƙaddamar da ƙaƙƙarfan warewa, tsananta wa Kiristoci, da ƙarfafa iko ta cikin bala'in tangena

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Gebre Mesqel Lalibela
translatedText: Mafi kyawun Gebre Mesqel

sourceText: Exiled due to a succession dispute and allegedly poisoned by his own half-sister, leading to a near-death experience.
translatedText: An yi gudun hijira saboda rikicin da ya biyo baya da kuma zargin ‘yar’uwarsa ta sa masa guba, lamarin da ya kai ga kusan mutuwa.

sourceText: His divine vision during his coma inspired him to return, claim the throne, and build the rock-hewn churches as an act of faith.
translatedText: Ganinsa na allahntaka a lokacin da yake suma ya motsa shi ya dawo, ya yi da'awar kursiyin, ya gina majami'u da aka sassaka dutse a matsayin aikin bangaskiya.

sourceText: 12th-13th Century
translatedText: Karni na 12-13

sourceText: Leo Africanus
translatedText: Leo Africanus

sourceText: Exile from his homeland of Granada, capture by pirates, and a forced conversion to Christianity in a foreign land.
translatedText: An yi hijira daga ƙasarsa ta Granada, 'yan fashi sun kama, da kuma tilasta musu tuba zuwa Kiristanci a wata ƙasa.

sourceText: He channeled his vast experiences and knowledge into his seminal work, 'Description of Africa', under the patronage of Pope Leo X, thus securing his legacy.
translatedText: Ya ba da ɗimbin abubuwan da ya samu da iliminsa a cikin aikinsa na farko, 'Description of Africa', ƙarƙashin jagorancin Paparoma Leo X, don haka ya tabbatar da gadonsa.

sourceText: 16th Century Renaissance
translatedText: Renaissance karni na 16

sourceText: Betrayed by deceptive treaties and overwhelmed by superior firepower, I witnessed the fall of my kingdom and the subjugation of my people.
translatedText: An ci amanata da yarjejeniyoyin yaudara, kuma ƙarfin wuta ya rinjaye ni, na shaida faɗuwar mulkina, da cin amanar jama'ata.

sourceText: Though my kingdom was lost, my story serves as a testament to the struggle for sovereignty and a warning against the insatiable greed of empire.
translatedText: Ko da yake sarautata ta yi hasarar, labarina ya zama shaida ga gwagwarmayar samun ikon mallaka da gargaɗi game da kwaɗayin daular.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Imprisonment in his youth, internal power struggles, and the threat of European colonialism.
translatedText: Daurin kurkuku a lokacin ƙuruciyarsa, gwagwarmayar mulkin cikin gida, da barazanar mulkin mallaka na Turai.

sourceText: Consolidating power, unifying the nation, and achieving a decisive victory against a foreign invader.
translatedText: Karfafa mulki, da hada kan al'umma, da samun gagarumin nasara a kan wani mamaya na kasashen waje.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Moshoeshoe I
translatedText: Moshoeshoe I

sourceText: Faced constant warfare and external threats during the chaos of the 'Difaqane' (the Crushing) caused by the rise of the Zulu Empire.
translatedText: Fuskantar yaƙe-yaƙe na yau da kullun da barazanar waje a lokacin hargitsi na 'Difaqane' (Murkushewar) da hawan Daular Zulu ya haifar.

sourceText: Utilized strategic defense from his Thaba Bosiu mountain fortress, diplomatic negotiation, and ultimately secured his nation's survival by requesting British protection.
translatedText: Ya yi amfani da dabarun tsaro daga sansaninsa na dutsen Thaba Bosiu, tattaunawar diflomasiyya, kuma ya tabbatar da rayuwar al'ummarsa ta hanyar neman kariyar Burtaniya.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Muhammad Ahmad
translatedText: Muhammad Ahmed

sourceText: Witnessing the corruption and oppressive taxes of the Turco-Egyptian rulers and the perceived decay of pure Islamic practice.
translatedText: Shaidawa cin hanci da rashawa da haraji na zalunci na sarakunan Turco-Masar da kuma fahimtar rugujewar tsarin addinin Musulunci.

sourceText: Embracing my divine calling as the Mahdi, unifying the tribes of Sudan under the banner of faith, and leading them to victory against foreign oppressors.
translatedText: Rungumar kirana na Ubangiji a matsayina na Mahdi, tare da hada kan kabilun Sudan karkashin tutar imani, tare da kai su ga cin nasara kan azzalumai na kasashen waje.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: The struggle against French colonialism, the fall of his kingdom, and his eventual exile and death far from his homeland.
translatedText: Yaki da Turawan mulkin mallaka na Faransa, da faduwar mulkinsa, da gudun hijira da mutuwarsa a nesa da kasarsa ta haihuwa.

sourceText: Even in defeat and exile, he became an enduring symbol of African independence and anti-colonial resistance.
translatedText: Ko a shan kaye da gudun hijira, ya zama alama mai ɗorewa ta 'yancin kai da adawa da mulkin mallaka na Afirka.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Saad Zaghloul
translatedText: Saad Zaghloul

sourceText: Repeated exile and imprisonment by the British for his nationalist activities.
translatedText: Maimaita gudun hijira da dauri da Birtaniya ke yi saboda ayyukansa na kishin kasa.

sourceText: Achieved Egypt's nominal independence through relentless negotiation and by galvanizing popular support.
translatedText: An sami 'yancin kai na Masar ta hanyar yin shawarwari ba tare da kakkautawa ba da kuma nuna goyon bayan jama'a.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Samori Toure
translatedText: Samori Toure

sourceText: Fighting the relentless advance of French colonialism while managing internal rivalries and the immense suffering of my people during our long march.
translatedText: Yaki da ci gaban mulkin mallaka na Faransa ba tare da ɓata lokaci ba tare da gudanar da kishiyoyi na cikin gida da gaggarumin wahalar da jama'ata suka sha a tsawon tafiyarmu.

sourceText: Through faith in Allah, military innovation, strategic retreats, and the unwavering spirit of my people, I forged an empire and resisted a global power for nearly two decades.
translatedText: Ta hanyar imani da Allah, sabbin ayyukan soji, ja da baya na dabaru, da ruhin mutanena, na kafa daula kuma na bijirewa ikon duniya na kusan shekaru ashirin.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Samuel Ajayi Crowther
translatedText: Samuel Ajayi Crowther

sourceText: Enslavement and racial prejudice
translatedText: Bauta da son zuciya

sourceText: Faith, education, and establishing identity through language
translatedText: Bangaskiya, ilimi, da kafa asali ta harshe

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Sayyid Mohammed Abdullah Hassan
translatedText: Sayyid Mohammed Abdullahi Hassan

sourceText: The division and oppression of his homeland by foreign colonial powers: the British, Italians, and Ethiopians.
translatedText: Rabe-rabe da zaluncin da Turawan mulkin mallaka na kasashen waje suka yi wa kasarsa: Turawan Ingila, Italiya, da Habasha.

sourceText: Unified Somali clans under the banner of Islam and nationalism, leading a 20-year armed resistance.
translatedText: Haɗin kai tsakanin dangin Somaliya a ƙarƙashin tutar Musulunci da kishin ƙasa, wanda ya jagoranci gwagwarmayar shekaru 20 da makamai.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Constant conflict with and condemnation from the Islamic scholars of Timbuktu, who labeled me a tyrant.
translatedText: Rikici akai-akai da kuma tofin Allah tsine daga malaman addinin Musulunci na Timbuktu, wadanda suka sanya ni azzalumi.

sourceText: By building an unstoppable military force and balancing the needs of my rural, traditionalist base with the urban Muslim elite.
translatedText: Ta hanyar gina rundunar soji da ba za a iya dakatar da ita ba da kuma daidaita bukatun karkarata, masu bin al'ada da manyan musulmin birni.

sourceText: 15th Century
translatedText: Karni na 15

sourceText: Sol Plaatje
translatedText: Hoton Sol

sourceText: Witnessing the systematic dispossession and oppression of his people through discriminatory laws like the 1913 Natives' Land Act.
translatedText: Shaidawa da tsare-tsare da zaluntar mutanensa ta hanyar dokokin wariya kamar Dokar Kasa ta 1913.

sourceText: Using his skills as a writer, journalist, and diplomat to document injustices, raise international awareness, and organize political resistance.
translatedText: Yin amfani da basirarsa a matsayin marubuci, ɗan jarida, da jami'in diflomasiyya don rubuta rashin adalci, wayar da kan duniya, da tsara adawar siyasa.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Witnessing my country descend into civil war due to my religious policies, and the immense loss of life among my own people.
translatedText: Shaidawa kasata ta fada cikin yakin basasa saboda manufofina na addini, da kuma asarar rayuka da aka yi a tsakanin jama’ata.

sourceText: By revoking my decree and abdicating the throne in favor of my son, I chose the unity of my people over my personal convictions and power.
translatedText: Ta wurin soke dokara, na bar gadon sarautar don in goyi bayan ɗana, na zaɓi haɗin kan jama'ata fiye da yarda da iko na.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karni na 17

sourceText: The encroachment of the Roman Empire and the personal injury I sustained in battle.
translatedText: Ƙullawar Daular Romawa da kuma raunin da na samu a yaƙi.

sourceText: Leading my armies to a strategic stalemate and negotiating a peace treaty that respected our sovereignty.
translatedText: Jagoranci dakaru na zuwa ga rikitacciyar dabara da yin shawarwari kan yarjejeniyar zaman lafiya da ta mutunta ikonmu.

sourceText: 1st Century BCE
translatedText: Karni na 1 KZ

sourceText: Ahmadu Bamba
translatedText: Ahmadu Bamba

sourceText: Misunderstanding and persecution by French colonial authorities, leading to years of exile.
translatedText: Rashin fahimta da zalunci daga hukumomin mulkin mallaka na Faransa, wanda ya kai ga gudun hijira na shekaru.

sourceText: Strength through faith, non-violent resistance, and building a community through education and labor.
translatedText: Ƙarfi ta bangaskiya, juriya mara tashin hankali, da gina al'umma ta hanyar ilimi da aiki.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: The powerful resistance from the priesthood of Amun, the societal upheaval my reforms caused, and the eventual erasure of my legacy.
translatedText: Ƙarfin juriya daga matsayin firist na Amun, da tashin hankalin al'umma da gyare-gyare na ya haifar, da kuma kawar da gadona daga ƙarshe.

sourceText: My unwavering commitment to my new faith and the construction of a new capital, Akhetaten (Amarna), as the center of this new world order.
translatedText: Ƙaƙwalwar da nake da ita ga sabon bangaskiyata da gina sabon babban birnin kasar, Akhetaten (Amarna), a matsayin cibiyar wannan sabuwar tsarin duniya.

sourceText: New Kingdom of Egypt, 18th Dynasty
translatedText: Sabuwar Masarautar Masar, Daular 18th

sourceText: Ahmad Baba al-Massufi
translatedText: Ahmad Baba al-Masufi

sourceText: The destruction of Timbuktu and the Songhai Empire by the Moroccan invasion; his arrest, the confiscation of his library, and his long exile in Marrakesh.
translatedText: Rushewar Timbuktu da Masarautar Songhai ta mamayewar Moroccan; kama shi, da kwace dakin karatunsa, da kuma zaman gudun hijira da ya dade a Marrakesh.

sourceText: His continued scholarship even in exile, his eventual release, and his return to a devastated Timbuktu where he continued to teach until his death.
translatedText: Ya ci gaba da karatunsa ko da a gudun hijira, a sake shi, da kuma komawa Timbuktu da ta lalace inda ya ci gaba da koyarwa har ya rasu.

sourceText: 16th-17th Century Songhai Empire
translatedText: Daular Songhai ta Karni na 16-17

sourceText: Ascending to the throne as a child, I ruled under the regency of my mother and faced the challenge of uniting a diverse kingdom while managing powerful external influences like the Roman Empire.
translatedText: Sa’ad da na hau kan karagar mulki sa’ad da nake yaro, na yi sarauta a ƙarƙashin sarautar mahaifiyata kuma na fuskanci ƙalubale na haɗa kan masarauta dabam-dabam sa’ad da na sarrafa iko mai ƙarfi kamar daular Roma.

sourceText: Guided by my tutor, Frumentius, I embraced a new faith which unified my people. Through strategic military campaigns and by controlling vital trade routes, I solidified Aksum's power and legacy.
translatedText: Jagorana ta mai koyarwa na, Frumentius, na rungumi sabon bangaskiya wanda ya haɗa kan mutanena. Ta hanyar yaƙin neman zaɓe na soja da kuma sarrafa mahimman hanyoyin kasuwanci, na ƙarfafa Aksum da ikonsa.

sourceText: 4th Century
translatedText: Karni na 4

sourceText: Anton Wilhelm Amo
translatedText: Anton Wilhelm Amo

sourceText: Forcible displacement from his homeland, racial prejudice, and isolation after the death of his patron.
translatedText: Tilastawa hijira daga mahaifarsa, wariyar launin fata, da keɓewa bayan mutuwar ubangidansa.

sourceText: Achieving academic excellence through noble patronage, earning a doctorate in philosophy, and pioneering as a lecturer in European universities.
translatedText: Samun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ilimi ta hanyar ɗorewa mai daraja, samun digiri na uku a fannin falsafa, da kuma yin ja-gora a matsayin malami a jami'o'in Turai.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karni na 18

sourceText: Athanasius of Alexandria
translatedText: Athanasius na Alexandria

sourceText: Enduring five exiles and constant persecution from Roman emperors and Arian-sympathizing clergy for defending orthodox doctrine.
translatedText: Jurewa ƴan gudun hijira biyar da tsanantawa akai-akai daga sarakunan Romawa da limaman Arian-tausayi don kare koyarwar Orthodox.

sourceText: Through unwavering conviction and prolific writing from exile, he rallied support for the Nicene cause, ultimately ensuring its triumph.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ba da tabbaci da kuma rubuce-rubuce masu yawa daga gudun hijira, ya ba da goyon baya ga manufar Nicene, yana tabbatar da nasara.

sourceText: 4th Century Roman Empire
translatedText: Daular Rum ta Karni na 4

sourceText: Cyril of Alexandria
translatedText: Cyril na Alexandria

sourceText: Intense theological battles, particularly with Nestorius, and navigating the political turmoil and violence of Alexandria.
translatedText: Yaƙe-yaƙe na tauhidi masu tsanani, musamman tare da Nestorius, da kuma zazzage hargitsin siyasa da tashin hankalin Alexandria.

sourceText: Vindication at the Council of Ephesus, where my Christological views were upheld, solidifying orthodox doctrine for generations.
translatedText: Hukunci a Majalisar Afisa, inda aka amince da ra'ayina na Kiristanci, yana ƙarfafa koyaswar koyarwa ta al'ada har tsararraki.

sourceText: 5th Century Roman Empire
translatedText: Daular Rum ta Karni na 5

sourceText: Amda Seyon I
translatedText: Amda Seyon I

sourceText: Constant threats from surrounding Muslim sultanates and internal rebellions from vassal lords.
translatedText: Barazana akai-akai daga kewayen sarakunan musulmi da tawaye na cikin gida daga iyayengiji.

sourceText: Through relentless military campaigns, strategic genius, and a policy of firm centralization, he crushed his enemies and expanded his empire.
translatedText: Ta hanyar yaƙin neman zaɓe na soji, ƙwararrun dabaru, da kuma tsarin tsagaita wuta, ya murkushe maƙiyansa, ya faɗaɗa daularsa.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karni na 14

sourceText: Abd al-Rahman al-Jabarti
translatedText: Abd al-Rahman al-Jabarti

sourceText: Witnessing the violent disruption of his society by foreign invasion and the subsequent brutal power struggles.
translatedText: Shaidawa tashe-tashen hankulan al'ummarsa ta hanyar mamayewar kasashen waje da kuma gwagwarmayar mulki na zalunci da suka biyo baya.

sourceText: By meticulously documenting events, he preserved the memory of his time and provided a powerful Egyptian perspective on a pivotal historical moment.
translatedText: Ta hanyar rubuta abubuwan da suka faru sosai, ya adana abubuwan tunawa da lokacinsa kuma ya ba da kyakkyawar hangen nesa na Masar akan wani muhimmin lokaci na tarihi.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 18th - Farkon Ƙarni na 19

sourceText: Abd al-Mu'min
translatedText: Abd al-Mu'min

sourceText: The immense burden of succeeding my master, Ibn Tumart, and the decades-long struggle to unite warring tribes and overthrow the decadent Almoravids.
translatedText: Babban nauyin magajin maigidana, Ibn Tumart, da gwagwarmayar da aka kwashe shekaru da dama ana yi na hada kan kabilun da ke fada da kifar da Almoravids.

sourceText: Through unwavering faith in the Almohad doctrine, strategic patience, and relentless military campaigns, I forged unity from chaos and built a righteous empire.
translatedText: Ta wurin bangaskiya mara kaushi ga koyarwar Almohad, haƙuri na dabaru, da yaƙin neman zaɓe na soji, na samar da haɗin kai daga hargitsi da gina daular adalci.

sourceText: 12th Century
translatedText: Karni na 12

sourceText: The loss of my country's independence and the long years of imprisonment and exile.
translatedText: Rasa 'yancin kai na kasata da tsawon shekarun daurin kurkuku da gudun hijira.

sourceText: Through unwavering faith, devotion to scholarship, and embracing a universal humanism that transcended conflict.
translatedText: Ta wurin bangaskiya mara kaushi, sadaukarwa ga malanta, da rungumar ɗan adamtaka ta duniya wacce ta zarce rikici.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Albert Luthuli
translatedText: Albert Luthuli

sourceText: Facing the brutality of the apartheid system, including being deposed as a chief, repeated arrests, and severe restrictions on my movement.
translatedText: Fuskantar zaluncin tsarin wariyar launin fata, gami da tsige shi a matsayin shugaban ƙasa, kama da aka yi akai-akai, da ƙuntatawa mai tsanani a kan motsi na.

sourceText: Drawing strength from my Christian faith and the unwavering spirit of my people, I led the ANC with a steadfast commitment to non-violent resistance.
translatedText: Da yake samun ƙarfi daga bangaskiyata ta Kirista da kuma ruhin jama'ata, na jagoranci ANC tare da jajircewa da jajircewa ba tare da tashin hankali ba.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karni na 20

sourceText: Yaa Asantewaa
translatedText: Ya Asantewaa

sourceText: The arrogance of the British governor, the demand for our sacred Golden Stool, and the initial fear of our chiefs.
translatedText: Girman kai na gwamnan Biritaniya, da neman alfarmar stool ɗinmu mai tsarki, da tsoron farko na sarakunanmu.

sourceText: By rallying the chiefs and the people, I took command of our army and led the fight to defend our sovereignty and culture.
translatedText: Ta hanyar tara sarakuna da jama'a, na ɗauki kwamandan sojojinmu kuma na jagoranci yaƙin don kare ikonmu da al'adunmu.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Origen of Alexandria
translatedText: Asalin asalin Alexandria

sourceText: Suffering persecution and torture for his faith, and the posthumous condemnation of his teachings as heresy.
translatedText: Wahalhalun zalunci da azabtarwa saboda imaninsa, da kuma hukuncin da aka yanke na koyarwarsa a matsayin bidi'a.

sourceText: Through immense scholarly dedication, he produced foundational works of theology and biblical criticism, leaving a profound intellectual and spiritual legacy.
translatedText: Ta wurin babban sadaukarwar ilimi, ya samar da ayyukan tushe na tiyoloji da sukar Littafi Mai Tsarki, yana barin babban gado na hankali da ruhi.

sourceText: 3rd Century Roman Empire
translatedText: Daular Rum ta Karni na 3

sourceText: Osei Tutu I
translatedText: Osei Tutu I

sourceText: The disunity and subjugation of the Akan peoples under the dominant Denkyira kingdom.
translatedText: Rashin haɗin kai da kuma mallake mutanen Akan a ƙarƙashin mulkin Denkyira.
